skill.name: canvas-design
skill.description: Create original visual design philosophies and express them as polished PNG or PDF canvas artwork with sparse typography, strong composition, and local font support.
skill.summary: Create design philosophies and polished visual canvas artwork as PNG or PDF files.
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and hardcoded URL hits are license-text false positives in bundled font and Apache license files. No prompt injection or malicious intent was found. The remaining concern is an open-ended instruction to download fonts, which can cause network access and processing of untrusted font files.

medium_findings.title: Open-Ended Font Download Instruction
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to download and use whatever fonts are needed. This is legitimate for a design workflow, but it can trigger network access and processing of untrusted font files unless the user approves the source.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction clearly requests downloads, so network access is a real possibility. Confidence is not higher because no exfiltration endpoint, forced command, or malicious destination is present.

low_findings.title: License URLs Are Benign Static Findings
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings occur in license text for the Apache License and SIL Open Font License. These are documentation references, not runtime network calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are standard license references. They do not appear in executable code and do not instruct the agent to transmit data.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Hits Are License Prose
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography and reconnaissance matches appear in legal definitions and license conditions. They are false positives caused by words inside bundled license documents and design prose.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative cited lines are non-executable license prose. No cryptographic API, scanner command, or system enumeration behavior is present at those locations.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Was Misread As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command finding points to inline Markdown around the local canvas-fonts directory. It is a documentation formatting marker, not Ruby backtick execution or a shell command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is Markdown instructional text and contains no executable script context. The backticks only format a relative directory path.

content.value_statement: Static visual work often lacks a clear creative direction before rendering. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to create a design philosophy and express it as a polished PNG or PDF canvas.
actual_capabilities: Creates a concise visual philosophy or movement brief before rendering artwork.
Guides creation of single-page PNG or PDF canvas outputs.
Emphasizes sparse typography, strong margins, and careful composition.
Uses bundled local font files for varied typography choices.
Supports multi-page visual series when the user requests more pages.
limitations: Requires a host tool that can generate or render PNG and PDF files.
External font downloads should use trusted sources and user approval.
Does not provide an interactive design editor or layout inspection UI.
Should not copy existing artists, brands, or copyrighted artwork.

use_cases.title: Create Event Posters
use_cases.description: Turn a short event concept into a refined poster with strong visual hierarchy and minimal text.

use_cases.title: Develop Editorial Art
use_cases.description: Create abstract single-page artwork for essays, reports, newsletters, or presentation covers.

use_cases.title: Prototype Brand Mood Pieces
use_cases.description: Explore visual direction, typography, color, and composition before committing to a full brand system.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Poster
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one visual artifact.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a single-page PNG poster for [topic]. Use this skill to make a design philosophy first, then render a polished visual canvas.

prompt_templates.title: Editorial Cover
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with audience and tone.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a PDF cover for [article or report]. The audience is [audience], and the tone should feel [tone]. Keep text minimal and visual-first.

prompt_templates.title: Brand Mood Artwork
prompt_templates.scenario: Structured creative direction for a product or campaign.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a visual mood piece for [brand or product]. Express [values] through composition, color, typography, and abstract imagery.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Page Art Series
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a cohesive series.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [number]-page PDF art series about [theme]. Each page should share one design philosophy while using distinct compositions.

output_examples.input: A poster for a small jazz night in a concrete basement venue.
output_examples.output: A concise design philosophy, followed by a dark, architectural PNG poster with sparse venue text and disciplined typography.

output_examples.input: A cover for a climate research memo aimed at city planners.
output_examples.output: A refined PDF cover using abstract mapping forms, restrained labels, and a calm color system.

output_examples.input: A three-page visual series for a ceramics studio launch.
output_examples.output: A short design manifesto and three related pages with quiet texture, spatial rhythm, and minimal brand wording.

best_practices: Provide the subject, audience, output format, and any required text before generation.
Ask for local bundled fonts first, and approve any external font source before download.
Review the final canvas for margins, overlap, readability, and copyright concerns.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to imitate a living artist or copy a known artwork.
Do not overload the canvas with paragraphs, dense instructions, or many slogans.
Do not download fonts or assets from unknown sources without review.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a design philosophy and then expresses it as a polished PNG or PDF canvas.

faq.question: Can it make multi-page documents?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports multi-page PDF or PNG series when the user asks for more pages.

faq.question: Does it include fonts?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill package includes many local font files and related license text.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: It can work with local fonts, but one instruction allows font downloads. Use trusted sources only.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium warning about optional external font downloads and untrusted assets.

faq.question: Can it copy an existing artist style?
faq.answer: No. The skill instructs users to create original visual designs and avoid copying artists.
